claim: The strongest long-term moat is transfer reliability across chips, not local optimization inside one repo.
mechanism: Local optimization can make the lab look polished, but transfer reliability is what makes it strategically valuable. When the lab can say that a methodology change improved multiple chips, survived contradiction review, and was promoted into packets with clear boundaries, it becomes the keeper of reusable portfolio intelligence. That is much harder to imitate than a polished local codebase.
boundary: This moat remains partly aspirational until the repo records more repeated cross-chip wins.
